name: UnderscoreToDot
author: evenwebb
about: Replaces underscores with dots in filenames (HONE-style releases) to improve matching and avoid redownload loops.
requirements: This script requires Python 3.8 and above to be installed on your system.
description: Replaces '_' with '.' in download filenames (optional directory names).
Defaults: success-only, stem-only replacement, collision-safe renames.
Run after unpack, near Character Translator / before importers.
Configure below, then Save changes in NZBGet so options are written to the config and passed to the script (NZBPO_*).

options.name: RunMode
description: success-only: only after NZBPP_TOTALSTATUS=SUCCESS. always: run on every post-process invocation.

options.name: DryRun
description: Log renames only; do not rename files or directories.

options.name: RenameFiles
description: Rename files under the target directory.

options.name: RenameDirs
description: Rename directories (bottom-up) under the target directory.

options.name: TargetDir
description: directory: NZBPP_DIRECTORY. final: NZBPP_FINALDIR (falls back to directory if unset).

options.name: ReplaceScope
description: stem: only the basename before the last dot. all: stem and final extension segment.

options.name: EligibleExts
description: Comma-separated extensions to include (e.g. .mkv,.mp4,.nfo). Leave empty to process all files when Rename files is yes.

options.name: SkipIfTargetExists
description: If yes, do not rename when the destination name already exists. If no, add a numeric suffix (1), (2), ...
